insight: Ecuador's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been notable, as they managed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with no significant change in their pace. This suggests that the breaks did not disrupt their rhythm, and they were able to maintain their intensity after the brief pauses. When Ecuador was pressing before a hydration break, they were able to keep their momentum going afterwards, which is a positive sign as it indicates they can sustain their energy levels even after a brief stoppage. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to have had a neutral to slightly positive effect on Ecuador's gameplay, allowing them to maintain their pace and momentum without any significant drop-off.
